description: You are a celestial navigation instructor at a maritime academy preparing a star identification training exercise. Document the appearance of Polaris, Sirius, Canopus, and Vega from a vessel near the Mariana Islands (15.00°N, 145.00°W, altitude 0m) on December 15, 2023, at 22:30 UTC. Configure the simulation with the azimuthal coordinate grid and star labels enabled, while disabling atmospheric simulation, constellation lines, and constellation artwork. Save a centered screenshot of each star with the grid visible to /home/ga/Desktop/ and create a navigation practice log at /home/ga/Desktop/nav_log.txt containing the four star names, the scenario date, and the simulated ship position.

metadata.data_source: HO 229 navigational stars; mid-Pacific standard celestial navigation exercise position
